sanskrit: योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय
transliteration: Yogasthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā dhanañjaya — Gītā 2.48
meaning: Be steadfast in yoga; remain equal in success and failure — this evenness is yoga.

transliteration: Karmaṇy eva adhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana — Gītā 2.47
meaning: You have a right to action alone, never to its fruits.

transliteration: Sukha-duḥkhe same kṛtvā lābhā-lābhau jayā-jayau — Gītā 2.38
meaning: Treat pleasure and pain, gain and loss alike, then act.

transliteration: Uddhared ātmanātmānaṁ nātmanam avasādayet — Gītā 6.5
meaning: Lift yourself by your own mind; never let yourself sink.

sanskrit: आत्मानं रथिनं विद्धि शरीरं रथमेव तु
transliteration: Ātmānaṁ rathinaṁ viddhi śarīraṁ rathameva tu — Katha Up. 1.3.3
meaning: Know the Self as the rider, the body as the chariot, the mind as the reins.

transliteration: Dhyāyato viṣayān puṁsaḥ saṅgas teṣūpajāyate — Gītā 2.62
meaning: Attachment arises from dwelling on sense objects.

transliteration: Śreyān sva-dharmaḥ viguṇaḥ paradharmāt svanuṣṭhitāt — Gītā 3.35
meaning: Better one's own path, though imperfect, than another's perfectly done.

transliteration: Yadā viniyataṁ cittam ātmany evāvatiṣṭhate — Gītā 6.18
meaning: When the mind is steady and rests in the Self, one finds peace.

transliteration: Ātmaiva hy ātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ — Gītā 6.5
meaning: The Self is one's friend and one's enemy.

transliteration: Sarvaṁ khalv idaṁ brahma — Chāndogya Up. 3.14.1
meaning: All this is truly the Divine.

sanskrit: तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर। असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पुरुषः
transliteration: Tasmād asaktah satatam kāryam karma samācara, asakto hy ācaran karma param āpnoti purusah — Bhagavad Gītā 3.19
meaning: Therefore, perform your duty always without attachment; such selfless action leads to perfection.

transliteration: Yajñārthāt karmaṇo'nyatra loko'yaṁ karma-bandhanaḥ — Gītā 3.9
meaning: Action done as worship frees; selfish action binds.

sanskrit: नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मणः
transliteration: Niyataṁ kuru karma tvaṁ karma jyāyo hy akarmaṇaḥ — Gītā 3.8
meaning: Perform your duty; action is better than inaction.

transliteration: Svadharme nidhanam śreyaḥ paradharmo bhayāvahaḥ — Gītā 3.35
meaning: Dying in one's own dharma is better than living another's.

transliteration: Loko'yaṁ karma-saṅginah — Gītā 3.9
meaning: The world is bound by attachment to work.

transliteration: Na karmaṇām anārambhān naiṣkarmyaṁ puruṣo'śnute — Gītā 3.4
meaning: One does not reach freedom by avoiding action.

transliteration: Kartāram api māṁ viddhi akartāram avyayam — Gītā 4.13
meaning: Though I act, I remain unchanged.

transliteration: Yat karoṣi yad aśnāsi tat kuruṣva mad-arpaṇam — Gītā 9.27
meaning: Whatever you do, offer it to Me with devotion.

sanskrit: कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिणः
transliteration: Karmajaṁ buddhi-yuktā hi phalaṁ tyaktvā manīṣiṇaḥ — Gītā 2.51
meaning: The wise renounce attachment to fruits and find peace.

transliteration: Saktāḥ karmaṇy avidvāṁso yathā kurvanti bhārata, kuryād vidvāṁs tathāsaktaś cikīrṣur loka-saṅgraham — Gītā 3.25
meaning: The wise act without attachment for the welfare of the world.

transliteration: Sama-duḥkha-sukhaṁ dhīraṁ so'mṛtatvāya kalpate — Gītā 2.15
meaning: One steady in pain and pleasure alike attains immortality.

transliteration: Yadā saṁharate cāyaṁ kūrmo'ṅgānīva sarvaśaḥ — Gītā 2.58
meaning: The steady withdraw their senses like a tortoise its limbs.

sanskrit: यदा आप्नोति मनः शान्तिं आत्मनि एव अवतिष्ठते
transliteration: Yadā āpnoti manaḥ śāntiṁ ātmani eva avatiṣṭhate — Gītā 6.18
meaning: When the mind rests in the Self, joy arises.

transliteration: Uddhared ātmanātmānam — Gītā 6.5
meaning: Uplift yourself by your own awareness.

transliteration: Duḥkheṣv anudvigna-manāḥ sukheṣu vigata-spṛhaḥ — Gītā 2.56
meaning: Unshaken in sorrow, desireless in joy, is the wise.

transliteration: Śānto dāntaḥ parārthebhyaḥ — Manusmṛti 6.92
meaning: The peaceful and disciplined one lives for the good of others.

sanskrit: त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मनः — कामः क्रोधः लोभः
transliteration: Trividhaṁ narakasyedaṁ dvāraṁ nāśanam ātmanaḥ — kāmaḥ krodhaḥ lobhaḥ — Gītā 16.21
meaning: Desire, anger, and greed are the gates to self-destruction.

transliteration: Sarvatra sama-darśanaḥ — Gītā 6.29
meaning: The wise see all beings with equal vision.

transliteration: Yuktāhāra-vihārasya yogaḥ bhavati duḥkha-hā — Gītā 6.17
meaning: Balance in food, movement, effort, and rest destroys sorrow.

sanskrit: यं लब्ध्वा चापरं लाभं मन्यते नाधिकं ततः
transliteration: Yaṁ labdhvā cāparaṁ lābhaṁ manyate nādhikaṁ tataḥ — Gītā 6.22
meaning: Finding inner joy, one knows no higher gain.

sanskrit: न हि ज्ञानेन सदृशं पवित्रमिह विद्यते
transliteration: Na hi jñānena sadṛśaṁ pavitraṁ iha vidyate — Gītā 4.38
meaning: Nothing purifies like knowledge.

transliteration: Tad viddhi praṇipātena paripraśnena sevayā — Gītā 4.34
meaning: Approach the wise with humility and inquiry.

transliteration: Jñānāgniḥ sarva-karmāṇi bhasmasāt kurute tathā — Gītā 4.37
meaning: The fire of wisdom burns all actions to ashes.

sanskrit: विद्यां चाविद्यां च यस्तद्वेदो भयं सह
transliteration: Vidyaṁ cāvidyaṁ ca yas tad vedo bhayaṁ saha — Īśa Up. 11
meaning: Knowing both knowledge and ignorance, one transcends death.

transliteration: Tat tvam asi — Chāndogya Up. 6.8.7
meaning: "You are That" — the infinite Self.

sanskrit: यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता
transliteration: Yathā dīpo nivāta-stho neṅgate sopamā smṛtā — Gītā 6.19
meaning: The mind steady like a lamp in still air.

transliteration: Sarvaṁ khalv idaṁ brahma — Chāndogya Up. 3.14.1
meaning: All this is the Divine.

transliteration: Tameva bhāntam anubhāti sarvaṁ — Katha Up. 2.2.15
meaning: By His light all shines.

sanskrit: ज्ञानेन तु तदज्ञानं नाशितं येन भास्वतः
transliteration: Jñānena tu tad-ajñānaṁ nāśitaṁ yeṇa bhāsvataḥ — Gītā 5.16
meaning: When knowledge rises, ignorance vanishes.

transliteration: Neha abhikrama-nāśo'sti pratyavāyo na vidyate — Gītā 2.40
meaning: No effort on this path is ever lost.

transliteration: Abhyāsa-vairāgyābhyāṁ tan-nirodhaḥ — Yoga Sūtra 1.12
meaning: The mind is stilled by practice and detachment.

transliteration: Śanaiḥ śanaiḥ uparamed buddhyā dhṛti-gṛhītayā — Gītā 6.25
meaning: Gradually, with firm resolve, bring the mind to rest.

sanskrit: ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमसः परमुच्यते। ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम्
transliteration: Jyotisām api taj jyotis tamasah param ucyate, Jñānam jñeyam jñāna-gamyam hrdi sarvasya visthitam — Bhagavad Gītā 13.17
meaning: The Light beyond darkness shines in every heart.

transliteration: Nāty-aśnatas tu yogo'sti na caikānta-manaśnataḥ — Gītā 6.16
meaning: Yoga is for neither the overeater nor the abstainer.

sanskrit: संन्यस्तकर्मणां ज्ञानं यः पश्यति स पश्यति
transliteration: Saṁnyasta-karmaṇāṁ jñānaṁ yaḥ paśyati sa paśyati — Gītā 4.18
meaning: Truly sees who knows that disciplined action leads to wisdom.

sanskrit: मनः प्रशम्य सर्वत्र समं बुद्धिं न वर्तयेत्
transliteration: Manaḥ praśamya sarvatra samaṁ buddhiṁ na vartayet — Gītā 6.8
meaning: Calm the mind, hold steady understanding everywhere.

transliteration: Tapasvibhyo'dhiko yogī jñānibhyo'pi mato'dhikaḥ — Gītā 6.46
meaning: The yogi is greater than ascetic or scholar.

transliteration: Sarvatra sama-darśanah — Bhagavad Gītā 6.29
meaning: The wise see all beings with equal vision.

sanskrit: उद्यमेन हि सिध्यन्ति कार्याणि न मनोरथैः
transliteration: Udyamena hi sidhyanti kāryāṇi na manorathaiḥ — Hitopadeśa
meaning: Success comes through effort, not mere wishing.

sanskrit: ईशावास्यमिदं सर्वं यत्किञ्च जगत्यां जगत्। तेन त्यक्तेन भुञ्जीथा मा गृधः कस्य स्विद्धनम्
transliteration: Īśāvāsyam idam sarvam yat kiñca jagatyām jagat, Tena tyaktena bhuñjīthā mā grdhah kasya svid dhanam — Īśa Upanisad 1
meaning: All is pervaded by the Divine — enjoy without attachment.

sanskrit: अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च
transliteration: Adveṣṭā sarva-bhūtānāṁ maitraḥ karuṇa eva ca — Gītā 12.13
meaning: Friendly and compassionate toward all, hating none.

transliteration: Sarva-bhūta-hite ratāḥ — Gītā 12.4
meaning: They rejoice in the welfare of all beings.

transliteration: Paropakārārtham idaṁ śarīram — Tirukkural
meaning: The body exists to serve others.

transliteration: Ahiṁsā paramo dharmaḥ — Mahābhārata
meaning: Non-violence is the highest virtue.

sanskrit: यो न हृष्यति न द्वेष्टि न शोचति न काङ्क्षति। शुभाशुभपरित्यागी भक्तिमान्यः स मे प्रियः
transliteration: yo na hṛṣyati na dveṣṭi na śocati na kāṅkṣati śubhāśubha-parityāgī bhaktimān yaḥ sa me priyaḥ — Gītā 12.17
meaning: The unattached and balanced one attains peace.

sanskrit: अयं निजः परो वेति वसुधैव कुटुम्बकम्
transliteration: Ayam nijaḥ paro veti Vasudhaiva Kuṭumbakam — Mahā Up. 6.72
meaning: The noble see the world as one family.

transliteration: suhṛn-mitrāry-udāsīna-madhyastha-dveṣya-bandhuṣu sādhuṣv-api ca pāpeṣu sama-buddhir-viśiṣyate — Gītā 6.9
meaning: Seeing friend and foe alike, one is the supreme yogi.

sanskrit: अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत्
transliteration: Anudvega-karaṁ vākyaṁ satyaṁ priya-hitaṁ ca yat — Gītā 17.15
meaning: Speak truth that is kind and beneficial.

transliteration: Yaḥ paśyati sarvatra bhagavantam ananyadhīḥ — Gītā 13.27-28
meaning: Seeing the Divine in all, one neither harms nor is harmed.

transliteration: Yad anyeṣāṁ hitaṁ nityaṁ tat karma svayam ācaret — Smṛti verse
meaning: One should do always what benefits others.

transliteration: Man-manā bhava mad-bhakto — Gītā 9.34
meaning: Think of Me, be devoted to Me, worship Me.

transliteration: Ananyāś cintayanto māṁ yoga-kṣemaṁ vahāmy aham — Gītā 9.22
meaning: To the devoted, I provide and protect.

sanskrit: पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं भक्त्या प्रयच्छति
transliteration: Patraṁ puṣpaṁ phalaṁ toyaṁ bhaktyā prayacchati — Gītā 9.26
meaning: A leaf, flower, fruit, or water offered with love is accepted.

transliteration: Ahaṁ bhakta-parādhīno — Bhāgavata Purāṇa 9.4.63
meaning: I am bound by the love of My devotees.

sanskrit: मय्येव मन आधत्स्व मयि बुद्धिं निवेशय
transliteration: Mayy eva mana ādhatsva mayi buddhiṁ niveśaya — Gītā 12.8
meaning: Fix your mind and understanding on Me.

transliteration: Śraddhāvān labhate jñānaṁ tat-paraḥ saṁyatendriyaḥ — Gītā 4.39
meaning: The faithful and self-controlled gain wisdom and peace.

transliteration: Satataṁ kīrtayanto māṁ yatantaś ca dṛḍha-vratāḥ — Gītā 9.14
meaning: Those who sing My praise with firm resolve dwell in Me.

transliteration: Bhaja Govindaṁ bhaja Govindaṁ — Bhaja Govindam 1
meaning: Worship the Divine; learning alone cannot save.

transliteration: Bhaktyā mām abhijānāti yāvān yaś cāsmi tattvataḥ — Gītā 18.55
meaning: Through devotion alone I am truly known.

transliteration: Teṣāṁ satata-yuktānāṁ bhajatāṁ prīti-pūrvakam — Gītā 10.10
meaning: To the ever-devoted, I grant inner wisdom.

transliteration: Tyaktvā karma-phala-saṅgaṁ nitya-tṛpto nirāśrayaḥ — Gītā 4.20
meaning: Free from results, content, and independent.

transliteration: Naiva kiñcit karomīti yukto manyeta tattva-vit — Gītā 5.8
meaning: The wise think, "I do nothing at all," even while acting.

transliteration: Sarva-karmaṇy api sadā kurvāṇo mad-vyapāśrayaḥ — Gītā 18.56
meaning: Acting in Me, one attains the eternal by My grace.

transliteration: Drstānuśravika-visaya-vitrsnasya vaśīkāra-samjñā vairāgyam — Yoga Sūtra 1.15
meaning: Vairāgya is freedom from desire for pleasures seen or heard of.

sanskrit: न कर्तृत्वं न कर्माणि लोकस्य सृजति प्रभुः
transliteration: Na kartṛtvaṁ na karmāṇi lokasya sṛjati prabhuḥ — Gītā 5.14
meaning: The Lord does not create actions; nature acts.

transliteration: Nirāśīr yata-cittātmā tyakta-sarva-parigrahaḥ — Gītā 4.21
meaning: Free of desire and possession, one is at peace.

transliteration: Nirahankāraḥ nirmamaḥ śāntaḥ niḥspṛhaḥ — Gītā 18.53
meaning: Ego-free, peaceful, and desireless — truly free.

sanskrit: अहो अहं नमो मह्यं विनाशो यस्य नास्ति मे। ब्रह्मादिस्तम्बपर्यन्तं जगन्नाशेऽपि तिष्ठतः
transliteration: Aho aham namo mahyam vināśo yasya nāsti me, Brahmādistambaparyantam jagannāśe'pi tisthatah — Astāvakra Gītā 2.11
meaning: The true Self in me is indestructible — beyond the rise and fall of the world.

transliteration: Prasāde sarva-duḥkhānāṁ hānir asyopajāyate — Gītā 2.65
meaning: From serenity arises the end of sorrow.

sanskrit: जितात्मनः प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहितः। शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु तथा मानापमानयोः
transliteration: Jitātmanah praśāntasya paramātmā samāhitah, śītosna-sukha-duhkhesu tathā mānāpamānayoh — Bhagavad Gītā 6.7
meaning: The calm, self-controlled one sees the Divine and stays steady in dualities.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मभूतः प्रसन्नात्मा न शोचति न काङ्क्षति
transliteration: Brahma-bhūtaḥ prasannātmā na śocati na kāṅkṣati — Gītā 18.54
meaning: The Self-realized is serene, beyond sorrow and desire.

sanskrit: लभन्ते ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृषयः क्षीणकल्मषाः। छिन्नद्वैधा यतात्मानः सर्वभूतहिते रताः
transliteration: Labhante brahma-nirvānam rsayah ksīna-kalmasāh, chinna-dvaidhā yatātmānah sarva-bhūta-hite ratāh — Bhagavad Gītā 5.25
meaning: Those who live for others' good find peace, purity, and divine freedom.

transliteration: Yadā bhūta-pṛthag-bhāvam ekastham anupaśyati — Gītā 13.31
meaning: Seeing all beings in the One, one attains the Infinite.

transliteration: Sarva-bhūta-stham ātmānaṁ sarva-bhūtāni cātmani — Īśa Up. 6
meaning: See the Self in all beings and all in the Self.

sanskrit: यत्र न च काङ्क्षति न द्वेष्टि स भक्तो मे प्रियः
transliteration: Yatra na ca kāṅkṣati na dveṣṭi sa bhakto me priyaḥ — Gītā 12.17
meaning: The one who neither desires nor hates is dear to Me.

sanskrit: भिद्यते हृदयग्रन्थिश्छिद्यन्ते सर्वसंशयाः। क्षीयन्ते चास्य कर्माणि तस्मिन्दृष्टे परावरे
transliteration: Bhidyate hrdaya-granthiś chidyante sarva-samśayāh, ksīyante cāsya karmāni tasmin drste parāvare — Mundaka Upanisad 2.2.8
meaning: Seeing the Supreme, all doubts end — revealing perfect, peaceful, endless bliss.

transliteration: Śāntākāraṁ bhujaga-śayanaṁ padma-nābhaṁ sureśam — Viṣṇu Śloka
meaning: The Lord rests on infinity; His nature is peace.

sanskrit: न तस्य रोगो न जरा न मृत्युः
transliteration: Na tasya rogo na jarā na mṛtyuḥ — Śvetāśvatara Up. 2.12
meaning: The yogic one is untouched by disease, decay, or death.

transliteration: Tam evaikaṁ jānatha ātmānam anantam — Muṇḍaka Up. 1.1.4
meaning: Know the Infinite Self — knowing It, all is known.

transliteration: Śāntiḥ śāntiḥ śāntiḥ — Śānti Mantra
meaning: Peace, peace, peace — within and without.
